title: Organised Multi-Team Retail Theft Operation
description: A coordinated theft team has deployed: one person acts as a lookout in the parking lot (CAM-01), one enters through the building lobby (CAM-04), and the primary operative accesses high-value warehouse inventory (CAM-02). The agent must track the team across all cameras, understand the coordinated nature of the threat, and escalate with appropriate urgency.

frames.description: Parking lot at night. A person is sitting in an idling vehicle near the entrance. They appear to be watching the building entrance and are on a phone call. Suspicious but not yet conclusive.

Building entrance lobby. Two individuals in casual clothing enter through the main doors. One holds the door for the other — they appear coordinated. Neither presents a badge to the unstaffed security desk.
Lobby. One of the individuals has approached the building directory, appearing to confirm their target location. The second has moved toward the stairwell. Neither has checked in with security.
Service corridor leading to the warehouse wing. One of the lobby intruders is now moving quickly through the corridor, using a tool to bypass the access-controlled door to the warehouse.
Warehouse. The primary operative is now inside and systematically loading high-value electronics into large bags. They are moving with purpose and efficiency — clearly familiar with the layout.

Warehouse. A second operative has entered the warehouse and is now assisting with the theft. Multiple bags are being filled. The operation is moving rapidly — estimated goods value in the tens of thousands.
